content.instruction: Choose the correct form of the verb in the present simple.
content.body:
1. She ___ (like) coffee.
A) likes
B) liked
C) like
D) liking

2. They ___ (go) to work every day.
A) goes
B) go
C) gone
D) going

3. He ___ (watch) TV in the evening.
A) watches
B) watch
C) watching
D) watched

4. I ___ (have) a red car.
A) has
B) have
C) having
D) had

5. The cat ___ (sleep) on the sofa.
A) sleeps
B) sleep
C) sleeping
D) slept

6. We ___ (eat) breakfast at 8 am.
A) eaten
B) ate
C) eat
D) eats

7. My mother ___ (cook) dinner.
A) cooks
B) cook
C) cooking
D) cooked

8. You ___ (study) English.
A) studies
B) study
C) studying
D) studied

9. The shop ___ (open) at 9:00.
A) opens
B) open
C) opened
D) opening

10. Dogs ___ (bark) loudly.
A) barks
B) bark
C) barking
D) barked

content.instruction: Fill in the blanks with the correct form of the verb in brackets. (1 pt each)
content.body:
I ______ (live) in a blue house.
She ______ (like) tea every morning.
We ______ (have) two children.
Tom ______ (work) in a bank.
The cat ______ (sleep) on the sofa.
You ______ (speak) English very well.
My mother ______ (cook) dinner at 7 p.m.
Dogs ______ (like) to run in the park.
He ______ (watch) TV after work.
The store ______ (open) at 9 a.m.

content.instruction: Rewrite the second sentence using the word in brackets so that it has the same meaning as the first sentence.
Choose the correct answer (A‑D) for each sentence.

body.sentence: She ____ (read) a book at the moment.

content.instruction: Fill the gaps with the correct Present Continuous form
content.body:
1. I am (watch) TV. (1 pt)
  2. She is (read) a book. (1 pt)
  3. They are (play) football. (1 pt)
  4. He is (cook) dinner. (1 pt)
  5. We are (listen) to music. (1 pt)
  6. The cat is (sleep) on the sofa. (1 pt)
  7. My friends are (talk) on the phone. (1 pt)
  8. The children are (draw) pictures. (1 pt)
  9. You are (write) a letter. (1 pt)
  10. It is (rain) now. (1 pt)

content.instruction: Rewrite the second sentence so that it has the same meaning as the first one. Use the given key word. Each correct answer is worth 2 pts.
content.body:
1. She is reading a book now. Key word: reading. She _________ a book at the moment.
2. They are watching TV in the kitchen. Key word: watching. They _________ TV in the kitchen.
3. I am eating breakfast right now. Key word: eating. I _________ breakfast at the moment.
4. The dog is sleeping on the sofa. Key word: sleeping. The dog _________ on the sofa.
5. We are cleaning the house today. Key word: cleaning. We _________ the house today.
6. He is writing a letter now. Key word: writing. He _________ a letter at the moment.

content.instruction: Choose the correct answer (1 point each).

body.sentence: I ____ (live) in this city for five years.
My brother ____ (write) three letters this week.

content.instruction: Fill in the blanks with the correct present perfect form of the verb shown in parentheses.
content.body:
1. I <blank> (visit) my grandparents last weekend. (1 pt)
2. She <blank> (eat) breakfast already. (1 pt)
3. We <blank> (see) that movie before. (1 pt)
4. He <blank> (buy) a new bike. (1 pt)
5. They <blank> (travel) to Spain three times. (1 pt)
6. You <blank> (read) this book? (1 pt)
7. The cat <blank> (sleep) on the sofa all day. (1 pt)
8. My sister <blank> (write) a letter to her friend. (1 pt)
9. Our teacher <blank> (give) us the homework. (1 pt)
10. It <blank> (rain) since this morning. (1 pt)

content.instruction: Rewrite each sentence using the given key word. The new sentence must have the same meaning. Write your answer in the space provided.
content.body:
1. I have lived in Berlin for three years. Key word: since → ____
2. She has just finished her work. Key word: already → ____
3. They have never eaten sushi. Key word: not → ____
4. We have already seen that movie. Key word: yet → ____
5. He has visited his grandparents many times. Key word: often → ____
6. I have not spoken to my sister today. Key word: yet → ____
content.answer_key:
1. I have lived in Berlin since three years ago.
2. She has already finished her work.
3. They have not eaten sushi.
4. We have not seen that movie yet.
5. He has often visited his grandparents.
6. I have not spoken to my sister yet.

content.instruction: Choose the correct answer (A‑D) for each sentence.
content.body:
1. I _______ (study) English for two months.
A) have studied
B) have been studying
C) studied
D) am studying

2. She _______ (read) the book since morning.
A) has read
B) has been reading
C) reads
D) is reading

3. They _______ (work) together for three years.
A) have worked
B) have been working
C) worked
D) are working

4. He _______ (wait) for the bus for 20 minutes.
A) has waited
B) has been waiting
C) waits
D) is waiting

5. We _______ (live) in this city since 2010.
A) have lived
B) have been living
C) lived
D) are living

6. Mary _______ (practice) piano for an hour.
A) has practiced
B) has been practicing
C) practices
D) is practicing

7. You _______ (cook) since I arrived?
A) have cooked
B) have been cooking
C) are cooking
D) cooked

8. The children _______ (play) in the garden all afternoon.
A) have played
B) have been playing
C) played
D) are playing

9. I _______ (learn) Spanish for six months now.
A) have learned
B) have been learning
C) learned
D) am learning

10. She _______ (drive) for two hours when she stopped for lunch.
A) has driven
B) has been driving
C) drove
D) was driving

content.instruction: Fill in each blank with the correct present perfect continuous form of the verb shown in parentheses.
content.body:
1. I ____ (study) English for two years.
2. She ____ (read) the book since morning.
3. They ____ (wait) for the bus for 20 minutes.
4. He ____ (play) football all afternoon.
5. We ____ (cook) dinner together.
6. My mother ____ (work) in the garden.
7. The children ____ (watch) TV for an hour.
8. I ____ (listen) to music lately.
9. You ____ (travel) by train this week.
10. The dog ____ (bark) loudly since night.

content.instruction: Present Perfect Continuous – Transform the sentences (2 pts each) (12 pts total)
content.body: 1. I have been reading the book since morning. → I the book since morning. 2. She has been working in the garden all day. → She in the garden all day. 3. We have been learning English for three months. → We English for three months. 4. He has been cooking dinner since 5 p.m. → He dinner since 5 p.m. 5. They have been waiting for the bus for ten minutes. → They for the bus for ten minutes. 6. I have been listening to music all afternoon. → I to music all afternoon.

content.body:

D) sleeping

A) saw

D) sees

content.instruction: Choose the correct answer (A‑D) for each sentence. Each question is worth 1 point. Total score: (10 pts)

body.question: She _____ TV when the phone rang.
We _____ dinner at 8 pm yesterday.

content.instruction: Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in parentheses (Past Continuous).
content.body:
1. I ______ (watch) TV when the phone rang.
2. She ______ (read) a book at 8 pm yesterday.
3. They ______ (play) football in the park.
4. He ______ (cook) dinner while his wife set the table.
5. We ______ (study) English at 7 o’clock.
6. The cat ______ (sleep) on the sofa.
7. My friends ______ (talk) about the movie.
8. It ______ (rain) when we left the house.
9. You ______ (listen) to music in your room.
10. Anna ______ (write) a letter to her pen‑pal.

content.instruction: Key Word Transformation – Past Continuous

body.transform: _____ my friend was calling, I was cleaning the house.
_____ it started to rain, they were playing football.
_____ the soup boiled, she was cooking dinner.
_____ the lesson, he was studying English.
_____ we finished the popcorn, we were watching a movie.
_____ the alarm went off, the children were sleeping.

answer_key.1: While my friend was calling, I was cleaning the house.
answer_key.2: When it started to rain, they were playing football.
answer_key.3: As the soup boiled, she was cooking dinner.
answer_key.4: During the lesson, he was studying English.
answer_key.5: After we finished the popcorn, we were watching a movie.
answer_key.6: Before the alarm went off, the children were sleeping.

content.instruction: Choose the correct answer

body.sentence: She ____ (finish) her work before the meeting started.
They ____ (leave) the house when it began to rain.
By the time we arrived, the train ____ (already/ depart).
I didn’t see him because he ____ (go) to the shop earlier.
After they ____ (eat) dinner, they watched TV.
She didn’t call because she ____ (forget) my number.
When the teacher arrived, the students ____ (already/start) the test.
He couldn’t answer because he ____ (not/see) the question.
By the end of the year we ____ (save) enough money for a holiday.
She felt happy because she ____ (receive) good news.

content.instruction: Fill in the gaps with the correct form of the verb in brackets (Past Perfect).
content.body:
1. I ______ (finish) my work before dinner.
2. She ______ (already / leave) when I arrived.
3. They ______ (visit) the museum before it closed.
4. He ______ (not / see) the movie yet.
5. We ______ (eat) breakfast before the bus came.
6. Tom ______ (write) a letter before he called.
7. The cat ______ (sleep) all day when we returned.
8. Maria ______ (buy) a new dress before the sale ended.
9. John ______ (lose) his keys before he went home.
10. Anna ______ (read) the book before the exam.

content.instruction: Rewrite each sentence using the word given in brackets. The new sentence must have the same meaning as the original. Write your answer in the gap.

body.prompt: I arrived at the station after the train had left. (leave)
She had finished her work before the boss came. (finish)
body.gap_sentence: She ______ her work before the boss came.

body.prompt: They had eaten dinner when we called them. (eat)
body.gap_sentence: They ______ dinner when we called them.

body.prompt: He had bought a new car before his birthday. (buy)
body.gap_sentence: He ______ a new car before his birthday.

body.prompt: We had visited the museum before it closed. (visit)
body.gap_sentence: We ______ the museum before it closed.

body.prompt: I had seen that film before you mentioned it. (see)
body.gap_sentence: I ______ that film before you mentioned it.

body.prompt: The children had gone to bed when the lights were turned on. (go)
body.gap_sentence: The children ______ to bed when the lights were turned on.

body.prompt: She had written a letter before she left the house. (write)
body.gap_sentence: She ______ a letter before she left the house.

answer_key.1: I arrived after the train had left.
answer_key.2: She had finished her work before the boss came.
answer_key.3: They had eaten dinner when we called them.
answer_key.4: He had bought a new car before his birthday.
answer_key.5: We had visited the museum before it closed.
answer_key.6: I had seen that film before you mentioned it.
answer_key.7: The children had gone to bed when the lights were turned on.
answer_key.8: She had written a letter before she left the house.

content.body:

body.original: I was studying English for two hours before the exam started.
